tossups.question: Two authors with this surname wrote a play in which a tramp meets Iris and Felix, who are butterflies. An author with this surname wrote a novel that ends with a metafictional conversation between the Author and the Writer imagining a war against the Lemurians. That novel uses Mr. Povondra’s newspaper clippings in the central chapter, which is titled “Up the Ladder of Civilization.” Two authors with this surname collaborated on the play From Insect Life. A novel by an author with this surname discusses a species discovered on the island of Tana Masa by Captain Van Toch. An author with this surname wrote about Harry Domin and Helena Glory, who represents the League of Humanity, in a play that popularized a word coined by his brother Josef. For 10 points, an author with what surname wrote War with the Newts and the play R.U.R.?

packet.name: M – Duke A, Duke B, Edinburgh, Houston, Toronto C

tossups.answer_sanitized: Capek ("CHAH-peck") [accept Karel Capek; accept Josef Capek]
tossups.question_sanitized: Two authors with this surname wrote a play in which a tramp meets Iris and Felix, who are butterflies. An author with this surname wrote a novel that ends with a metafictional conversation between the Author and the Writer imagining a war against the Lemurians. That novel uses Mr. Povondra's newspaper clippings in the central chapter, which is titled "Up the Ladder of Civilization." Two authors with this surname collaborated on the play From Insect Life. A novel by an author with this surname discusses a species discovered on the island of Tana Masa by Captain Van Toch. An author with this surname wrote about Harry Domin and Helena Glory, who represents the League of Humanity, in a play that popularized a word coined by his brother Josef. For 10 points, an author with what surname wrote War with the Newts and the play R.U.R.?

tossups.question: The protagonist of a play by this author works for Sigelius at the Lilienthal Clinic and is killed by a crowd shouting, "We want war!" In another play by this author, a woman who uses pseudonyms with the initials "E. M." proves pivotal to the case Gregor v. Prus. Galen fails to save the Marshal from the "white disease" in another play by this author. This author created a representative from the Humanity League who burns a secret formula in one play, and wrote about an immortality-granting potion in The Makropulos Secret. In this author's most famous play, Alquist calls Primus and Helena the new Adam and Eve. In that play by this author, Harry Domin manages a company that creates the title artificial people, who stage a revolt against humankind. For 10 points, name this Czech playwright of R.U.R.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Karel Capek (CHAH-pek) [The first play is Matka.]
tossups.question_sanitized: The protagonist of a play by this author works for Sigelius at the Lilienthal Clinic and is killed by a crowd shouting, "We want war!" In another play by this author, a woman who uses pseudonyms with the initials "E. M." proves pivotal to the case Gregor v. Prus. Galen fails to save the Marshal from the "white disease" in another play by this author. This author created a representative from the Humanity League who burns a secret formula in one play, and wrote about an immortality-granting potion in The Makropulos Secret. In this author's most famous play, Alquist calls Primus and Helena the new Adam and Eve. In that play by this author, Harry Domin manages a company that creates the title artificial people, who stage a revolt against humankind. For 10 points, name this Czech playwright of R.U.R.

tossups.question: In a novel by this author, Abe Loeb and Sweetiepie Li visit a lagoon on their yacht, the Gloria Pickford. He satirized Nazi Germany in a play in which Dr. Galen cures a disease which only affects people over 45. In one of his novels, the creation of a revolutionary form of energy has the unintended side effect of producing a spiritual substance called the “absolute.” This author of The White Disease wrote a novel in which G. H. Bondy exploits the title animals, which are discovered by Captain van Toch. In his most famous play, Helena meets with Fabry and Dr. Gall as a representative of the League of Humanity and advocates freeing the title beings, who eventually rise up and kill all the humans except Alquist. For 10 points, name this Czech author who wrote War with the Newts and coined the word “robot” in his play R.U.R.
tossups.question_sanitized: In a novel by this author, Abe Loeb and Sweetiepie Li visit a lagoon on their yacht, the Gloria Pickford. In his most famous play, Helena meets with Fabry and Dr. Gall as a representative of the League of Humanity and advocates freeing the title beings, who eventually rise up and kill all the humans except Alquist. For 10 points, name this Czech author who wrote War with the Newts and coined the word "robot" in his play R.U.R.

tossups.question: In one of his stories, a clerk is marginalized from society when his friends learn that he owns a 7-headed hydra. He included “The Bank Clerk’s Tale” in his collection Nine Fair Tales: And One More Thrown in For Good Measure. One of this author’s characters is spared from death because of his hands and later tells Damon and Radius that he can’t make the secret of life in a test tube. In one of his plays, the longstanding court case of Gregor v Prus is manipulated by (*) Emila Marty, an opera singer who is 300 years old. This author of The Makropulos Case wrote a play in which Alquist’s creations destroy humanity, including Harry Domin and Dr. Gall. For 10 points, name this author who introduced the word “robot” in his play R.U.R.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Karel Capek [pronounced "cha-pek," but be really lenient.]
tossups.question_sanitized: In one of his stories, a clerk is marginalized from society when his friends learn that he owns a 7-headed hydra. He included "The Bank Clerk's Tale" in his collection Nine Fair Tales: And One More Thrown in For Good Measure. One of this author's characters is spared from death because of his hands and later tells Damon and Radius that he can't make the secret of life in a test tube. In one of his plays, the longstanding court case of Gregor v Prus is manipulated by (*) Emila Marty, an opera singer who is 300 years old. This author of The Makropulos Case wrote a play in which Alquist's creations destroy humanity, including Harry Domin and Dr. Gall. For 10 points, name this author who introduced the word "robot" in his play R.U.R.

tossups.question: In one play by this author, the Marshal in charge of the unnamed setting doesn’t care that people over the age of forty-five are dying to the title ailment, because the youthful members of the military are unaffected. In another play, this author wrote of a technological advance that allowed humans to annihilate matter to generate energy, but the (*)byproduct causes world wide strife. This author of The White Disease and The Absolute at Large created the Captain von Toch who discovers the title creatures of another work near Sumatra before those creatures exploited by humans. In his most famous work, this author created Harry Domain, the manager of the title company that produces automata. For 10 points, name this Czech author of War with the Newts and R.U.R.
tossups.question_sanitized: In one play by this author, the Marshal in charge of the unnamed setting doesn't care that people over the age of forty-five are dying to the title ailment, because the youthful members of the military are unaffected. This author of The White Disease and The Absolute at Large created the Captain von Toch who discovers the title creatures of another work near Sumatra before those creatures exploited by humans. In his most famous work, this author created Harry Domain, the manager of the title company that produces automata. For 10 points, name this Czech author of War with the Newts and R.U.R.

In this Simone de Beauvoir novel, Raymond Fosca lives from the 13th century to the 1940s and fears being the last being left alive on Earth after drinking a beggar’s immortality potion.
answers: Virginia Woolf [or Adeline Virginia Woolf; or Adeline Virginia Stephen]

parts_sanitized: Name this novel in which a group of reformatory boys are left in an abandoned village after the plague strikes and the villagers take off.

This Oe story is about a 35-year-old man who wears underwater goggles covered with cellophane in his hospital bed, where he hopes that he has cancer but doesn't know if he does, and remembers his time as a youth during the war hearing the Emperor's voice.

This author wrote about a leprosy disease, which kills all people over 45 unless they're healed by Doctor Galen, in his play The White Plague. He also wrote a novel in which humans engage in a war with Newts, after forming the Salamander Syndicate.

bonuses.leadin: In collaboration with his brother Josef, this author wrote a play in which a tramp dreams that human vices are represented as dung beetles, ants, and butterflies. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author of Insect Play, who is more famous for a play in which a group of South Pacific amphibians grow exponentially more intelligent and threaten the human race globally.
Capek is most famous for R.U.R, in which he coined this word for a race of automatons including Sulla, Marius, Radius, Proteus, Damon, and Helena, borrowing the Czech word for laborer.

Random countries invade hilariously distant territories in this critique of capitalism by Capek, in which the engineer Rudolph Marek invents the Karburator, a machine which uses the title residue-less energy.
answers: The Absolute at Large [or The Factory of Absolute; or Tovarna na absolutno]
answers_sanitized: The Absolute at Large [or The Factory of Absolute; or Tovarna na absolutno]
